It offers interactive lessons, coding challenges, and certifications in web development, programming, and computer science. One of the largest and most popular free learning resources in the developer community.

technologies.name: Public-Apis
technologies.description: A curated, community-maintained collection of free APIs for developers to use in their projects. Covers various categories including animals, anime, finance, games, and more. Serves as a go-to resource for developers seeking public APIs.

technologies.name: Free-Programming-Books
technologies.description: A curated collection of freely available programming books covering various languages and topics. Maintained on GitHub, it serves as a comprehensive resource for developers seeking free learning materials.

technologies.name: Developer-Roadmap
technologies.description: Interactive roadmaps and educational guides helping developers navigate career paths in software development, covering various roles and technologies.

technologies.name: Awesome-Python
technologies.description: A curated, opinionated list of awesome Python frameworks, libraries, software and resources. This community-maintained collection serves as a comprehensive reference for Python developers seeking quality tools and learning resources.

watchlist.name: Python
watchlist.description: All Algorithms implemented in Python

watchlist.name: build-your-own-x
watchlist.description: A comprehensive collection of tutorials and guides that teach programming concepts by rebuilding popular technologies (databases, neural networks, git, etc.) from scratch. Used by millions of developers for learning and skill development.

watchlist.name: awesome
watchlist.description: The 'awesome' movement is a community-driven curation format for organizing and sharing curated lists of resources on various topics. Originally started by Sindre Sørhus, it has become a standard convention on GitHub for discovering tools, libraries, learning resources, and other collections.
